content:
===BEGIN===
Inside your body, there are tiny cells that do lots of important jobs. Each cell is like a little factory that makes proteins, which help your body grow and stay healthy. But how does the cell know what kind of protein to make? This is where mRNA comes in!

mRNA is like a special messenger inside the cell. It carries instructions from the cell’s control center, called the nucleus, to the part of the cell where proteins are made. These instructions are written in a code, kind of like a secret recipe.

First, the cell’s control center makes a copy of the protein recipe using mRNA. Then, the mRNA travels to a protein factory part of the cell called the ribosome. The ribosome reads the mRNA’s instructions and puts together the right building blocks to make the protein.

So, mRNA is very important because it carries the instructions that help cells make everything your body needs to work and grow!  
===END===
